WITN04660100
WITN04660100
DocuSign Envelope ID: 0B8D9A6F-D117-40AC-B8CA-9EFEBC3C2B1D

Date
Event
We offered the use of our firm's London office to help save the
venue costs. [POL00070832]
Mr Castleton's solicitors did not respond, nor did they raise the
question of mediation once disclosure had taken place.
5 September 2006
I had a without prejudice conversation with Mr Turner of Rowe
Cohen [POL00072614]. I raised ADR. Mr Turner said that
"they hadn't ruled out ADR but now was not the time."
7 September 2006
My attendance note of a call with Tom Beezer [POL00069612]
records that I had spoken to Mr Castleton's solicitors earlier that
week. "They told me that they had not ruled out ADR but believed
the whole case would turn on what experts say and there was no
purpose in having ADR until their expert (hopefully for them)
comes up with something to win the case for them."
11 September
2006
My attendance note [POL00069622] of the Conference on 11
September 2006 records Counsel's advice was that "settlement
was attractive from both sides" "Mandy Talbot agreed that on a
without prejudice basis I would say to Mr Castleton's solicitors
that I was surprised that they didn't accept our without prejudice
offer."

Page 109 of 194
